title: Rust `as` casts never panic: ints truncate, float-to-int saturates
rule: Unlike arithmetic, the `as` operator never panics in any build profile. Integer narrowing silently truncates the low bits (300 as u8 == 44, -1 as u8 == 255), and since Rust 1.45 float-to-int `as` saturates instead of being UB (1e9 as u8 == 255, -5.0 as u8 == 0, NaN as u8 == 0). This is treacherous next to the debug-overflow panic: the same out-of-range value that would panic via + quietly produces a wrong number via `as`. For range-checked conversion use TryFrom/TryInto (u8::try_from(x)) and handle the Err, rather than reaching for `as`.
triggers: cast produces unexpected number instead of error
no panic on out of range cast in Rust
TryFrom vs as for integer conversion
